pages.text: Vanilla Minecraft seeds villages in five biomes: $(bold)Plains$(), $(bold)Desert$(), $(bold)Savanna$(), $(bold)Taiga$(), and $(bold)Snowy$(). Each one has its own architecture, palette, and crops.$(br2)Novus adds many more.
pages.title: The Vanilla Five

pages.text: $(bold)Repurposed Structures$() generates village variants in roughly $(bold)twenty$() additional biomes: cherry grove, bamboo jungle, birch forest, dark forest, jungle, badlands, oak forest, giant taiga, mountains, mushroom fields, swamp, and even the $(italic)ocean$() — built on stilts above the waves.$(br2)Each variant uses the local wood and local stone, so a jungle village looks jungle, a mountain village looks alpine.
pages.title: Repurposed Villages

pages.text: $(bold)Villages in the Nether.$() Repurposed Structures adds $(bold)Crimson$() and $(bold)Warped$() villages too — piglin-built towns in the crimson and warped forest biomes. Their trades skew toward netherrack and soul-soil materials; their roofs are nether-brick.$(br2)Treat them carefully — they generate small numbers of hostile piglins as inhabitants.
pages.title: Nether Villages

pages.text: $(bold)Finding a village.$() Hold an $(italic)Explorer's Compass$() and right-click — a menu appears listing every structure type in the world. Pick $(italic)Village$() (or any specific variant), and the compass points the bearing and shows the distance. The compass costs nothing to use and has no cooldown; carry one always.
pages.title: Explorer's Compass

pages.text: $(bold)Waystones in villages.$() Many vanilla and modded village types generate a $(bold)Waystone$() — a tall standing stone, usually near the centre. Right-click any waystone to bind it; once bound, you can teleport to it from any other waystone, or from a Warp Plate / Warp Stone.$(br2)$(italic)A bound village waystone is the cheapest fast-travel network in the game$() — every village you find is a future shortcut home.
pages.title: Waystones

pages.text: $(bold)Quark's More Villagers.$() Vanilla gives villagers five biome-skins: plains, desert, savanna, taiga, snowy. $(italic)Quark$() adds two more — $(bold)Ocean$() and $(bold)Beach$() — so the villagers who live in coastal and ocean-village builds look the part. Same trades, new faces.
pages.title: More Villagers
